source_jobs.title: Staff Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT KUESKI


At Kueski, we're dedicated to improving the financial lives of people in Mexico. Since 2012, we've been the leading buy now, pay later (BNPL) and online consumer credit platform in Latin America, known for our innovative financial services. Our flagship product, Kueski Pay, provides seamless payment solutions for both online and in-store transactions, establishing itself as the preferred option for nearly 30% of Mexico's top e-commerce merchants. Notably, we were the first to introduce BNPL on Amazon Mexico.

We're a tech company with a culture geared toward innovation, collaboration, and impact, fostering a strong, diverse, and inclusive workplace. Our commitment to excellence and ethical business practices has earned us multiple industry recognitions. In 2024, we were named one of the World’s Top FinTech Companies by CNBC and recognized as one of the most ethical companies in Mexico by AMITAI. Additionally, we were certified as a Best Place to Work for LGBTQ+ Equality by HRC Equidad MX 2025 and ranked among the Best Companies for Female Talent by EFY.



POSITION



Kueski is seeking a Staff-level Data Engineer to drive the technical strategy, architecture, and long-term evolution of our data platform.

This role is ideal for a deeply technical leader who thrives in complex, ambiguous environments and can deliver scalable data solutions from problem definition through production.

As a key leader in Data Engineering, you will own platform direction, establish engineering standards, and lead large-scale initiatives across batch, streaming, AI-centric, and governance capabilities.

Reporting to the Sr Manager of Data Engineering, you will partner across Data Science, ML, Analytics, Platform, and Product to build reliable, high-impact data systems.

You will mentor engineers at all levels, elevate technical quality, and make architecture decisions that directly support Kueski’s business objectives and long-term platform health.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




TECHNICAL STRATEGY, ARCHITECTURE & PLATFORM ROADMAP

 - Define and drive the data engineering technical strategy, architecture decisions, and platform roadmap aligned to company objectives

 - Lead and deliver large-scale, complex data initiatives—spanning multiple teams and iterations—from ambiguous problem definition through production deployment

 - Design robust, scalable data architectures (batch and streaming) that support Kueski's long-term business needs at scale

 - Demonstrated success shaping and executing an AI-centric data strategy that leverages the latest AI technologies to accelerate value delivery, enable trusted self-service data consumption, and strengthen data quality, governance, and organizational decision-making.

 - Identify the limits of existing tools or processes; lead the design and build of new capabilities when current solutions fall short


ENGINEERING EXCELLENCE, STANDARDS & PLATFORM RELIABILITY

 - Shape, standardize, and champion data engineering methodologies, best practices, and technical standards for the team and department

 - Develop and own CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure-as-code for reliable, automated data platform operations

 - Drive data quality, observability, and governance programs across the data platform

 - Apply data cleansing techniques to facilitate data consumption and quality across the platform


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP & ORGANIZATIONAL IMPACT

 - Partner cross-functionally with Data Science, ML, Analytics, Platform, and Product teams to deliver data-driven solutions end-to-end

 - Represent data engineering in cross-organizational initiatives; support and lead efforts outside the core area of responsibility


MENTORSHIP, TECHNICAL ENABLEMENT & TEAM ELEVATION

 - Mentor and guide Data Engineers at all levels; constructively challenge assumptions and elevate team quality through code review, pairing, and coaching




EXPERIENCE



 - Deep expertise in data engineering at scale: architecture design,

source_jobs.title: Paid Analytics Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT KUESKI

At Kueski, we're dedicated to improving the financial lives of people in Mexico. Since 2012, we've been the leading buy now, pay later (BNPL) and online consumer credit platform in Latin America, known for our innovative financial services. Our flagship product, Kueski Pay, provides seamless payment solutions for both online and in-store transactions, establishing itself as the preferred option for nearly 30% of Mexico's top e-commerce merchants. Notably, we were the first to introduce BNPL on Amazon Mexico.

We're a tech company with a culture geared toward innovation, collaboration, and impact, fostering a strong, diverse, and inclusive workplace. Our commitment to excellence and ethical business practices has earned us multiple industry recognitions. In 2024, we were named one of the World’s Top FinTech Companies by CNBC and recognized as one of the most ethical companies in Mexico by AMITAI. Additionally, we were certified as a Best Place to Work for LGBTQ+ Equality by HRC Equidad MX 2025 and ranked among the Best Companies for Female Talent by EFY.




POSITION

Kueski is seeking a senior Growth Marketing Analytics leader to own marketing measurement end-to-end from attribution infrastructure and implementation to advanced analysis and strategic recommendations. This role requires a highly autonomous professional who can build scalable analytics frameworks, uncover second-order business impacts beyond standard attribution models, and translate complex data into actionable growth decisions.

This role is ideal for an analytical expert who thrives in high-ownership environments, enjoys connecting marketing activities to business outcomes, and can partner cross-functionally with marketing, sales, and analytics teams to optimize investment decisions and accelerate sustainable growth.




KEY RESPONSABILITIES




ATTRIBUTION STRATEGY & MEASUREMENT

 - Design, implement, and continuously refine marketing attribution models across all channels, ensuring they reflect the true complexity of the customer journey.

 - Develop a framework to identify and account for second-order effects initiatives whose impact on brand awareness, funnel priming, or downstream conversion isn't directly attributable but is strategically significant.

 - Define incrementality measurement approaches (geo holdouts, intent-to-treat analyses, synthetic controls) to evaluate the true causal impact of marketing investments beyond last-touch or even multi-touch models.

 - Own attribution methodology decisions and communicate trade-offs clearly to stakeholders and leadership.


TECHNICAL IMPLEMENTATION & DATA INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Lead the implementation and maintenance of tracking infrastructure: pixel setup, tag management (Google Tag Manager or equivalent), and event tracking across web and app.

 - Design and implement server-to-server (S2S) integrations to improve signal quality, reduce data loss from browser restrictions, and enhance campaign optimization inputs for paid media platforms.

 - Audit and troubleshoot data discrepancies across tracking systems, ensuring data accuracy and integrity end-to-end.

 - Manage and evolve the marketing analytics stack (GA4, Amplitude, third-party attribution tools, Databricks) and stay current on emerging measurement technologies and privacy-driven changes.

 - Partner with Data Engineering and Product to improve event schemas, tracking coverage, and data pipeline reliability.


DATA ANALYSIS & SELF-SUFFICIENT INSIGHT GENERATION

 - Pull, structure, and analyze your own data independently building the analyses needed to reach conclusions and push initiatives forward, without relying on others to surface information.

 - Develop and maintain dashboards and reports that give clear, actionable visibility into campaign performance, channel ROI, and attribution trends.

 - Identify patterns and anomalies proactively; bring forward recommendations with a clear point of view, not just observations.

 - Use adva

source_jobs.title: Director of Growth Marketing (Acquisition & Lifecycle)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT KUESKI

At Kueski, we're dedicated to improving the financial lives of people in Mexico. Since 2012, we've been the leading buy now, pay later (BNPL) and online consumer credit platform in Latin America, known for our innovative financial services. Our flagship product, Kueski Pay, provides seamless payment solutions for both online and in-store transactions, establishing itself as the preferred option for nearly 30% of Mexico's top e-commerce merchants. Notably, we were the first to introduce BNPL on Amazon Mexico.

We're a tech company with a culture geared toward innovation, collaboration, and impact, fostering a strong, diverse, and inclusive workplace. Our commitment to excellence and ethical business practices has earned us multiple industry recognitions. In 2024, we were named one of the World’s Top FinTech Companies by CNBC and recognized as one of the most ethical companies in Mexico by AMITAI. Additionally, we were certified as a Best Place to Work for LGBTQ+ Equality by HRC Equidad MX 2025 and ranked among the Best Companies for Female Talent by EFY.




POSITION

Kueski is seeking a VP-level Director of Acquisition & Lifecycle Marketing to own end-to-end growth performance, roadmap execution, and scalable acquisition and retention engines. This role is ideal for a hands-on growth leader with deep unit economics expertise (CAC, LTV, payback) who can drive experimentation, optimize investment, and build high-performing teams. As a key leader, you will partner closely with the Head of Growth and executive stakeholders to shape strategy, enforce operational discipline, and deliver measurable, sustainable growth impact.




KEY RESPONSABILITIES




GROWTH STRATEGY & OWNERSHIP

 - Define and own the acquisition and lifecycle marketing strategy across the full customer journey, from first touch to long-term retention and monetization.

 - Translate company and growth objectives into clear acquisition targets, lifecycle goals, and investment strategies.

 - Own performance outcomes and efficiency metrics, including CAC, LTV, payback, conversion rates, and retention KPIs.


ACQUISITION LEADERSHIP

 - Personally accountable for media planning, buying, and execution across online and offline channels (digital, TV, OOH, partnerships), including budget allocation, negotiation, and performance optimization.

 - Lead multi-channel acquisition strategy across paid media, partnerships, affiliates, SEO/ASO, and other scalable growth channels.

 - Ensure strong alignment between Brand and performance efforts, balancing short-term efficiency with long-term brand equity.

 - Oversee experimentation frameworks to continuously optimize channel mix, messaging, targeting, and creative performance.


LIFECYCLE & ENGAGEMENT STRATEGY

 - Own lifecycle marketing strategy with deep hands-on work across activation, engagement, retention, cross-sell, and reactivation.

 - Partner with Product and Data to define customer segmentation, personalization strategies, and trigger-based communications.

 - Ensure lifecycle programs drive meaningful customer value while supporting revenue and retention objectives.


PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT & DECISION-MAKING

 - Establish clear performance management frameworks, dashboards, and reporting cadences to track growth and lifecycle outcomes.

 - Use data and insights to guide prioritization, tradeoffs, and investment decisions.

 - Lead post-mortems and learning cycles, translating results into scalable playbooks and best practices.


OPERATING MODEL & CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP

 - Partner with Marketing Operations to define scalable processes, planning cycles, and experimentation governance.

 - Collaborate with Product, Analytics, Finance, Risk, and Legal to ensure compliant, efficient, and customer-centric execution.

 - Serve as a senior thought partner to leadership, providing clear recommendations backed by data.


TEAM & AGENCY LEADERSHIP

 - Build, lead, and develop high-performing acquisition and

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT KUESKI

At Kueski, we're dedicated to improving the financial lives of people in Mexico. Since 2012, we've been the leading buy now, pay later (BNPL) and online consumer credit platform in Latin America, known for our innovative financial services. Our flagship product, Kueski Pay, provides seamless payment solutions for both online and in-store transactions, establishing itself as the preferred option for nearly 30% of Mexico's top e-commerce merchants. Notably, we were the first to introduce BNPL on Amazon Mexico.

We're a tech company with a culture geared toward innovation, collaboration, and impact, fostering a strong, diverse, and inclusive workplace. Our commitment to excellence and ethical business practices has earned us multiple industry recognitions. In 2024, we were named one of the World’s Top FinTech Companies by CNBC and recognized as one of the most ethical companies in Mexico by AMITAI. Additionally, we were certified as a Best Place to Work for LGBTQ+ Equality by HRC Equidad MX 2025 and ranked among the Best Companies for Female Talent by EFY.




POSITION

Kueski is seeking a Senior Product Account Manager to own and grow a strategic enterprise partnership, leading commercial performance, executive relationships, and cross-functional initiatives that drive business impact.

This role is ideal for a strategic, execution-driven leader who thrives in complex environments and excels at influencing stakeholders, driving growth, and delivering operational excellence.




KEY RESPONSABILITIES




ACCOUNT OWNERSHIP & STRATEGY

 - Own the overall success, performance, and growth of a key enterprise account, including revenue, profitability, and customer experience metrics

 - Develop and execute the account strategy, identifying opportunities for expansion, optimization, and long-term value creation

 - Act as the primary point of contact for senior stakeholders, building strong, trust-based relationships at executive levels

 - Lead business reviews (QBRs/MBRs), aligning on performance, insights, and future roadmap


COMMERCIAL & RELATIONSHIP MANAGEMENT

 - Drive commercial negotiations, renewals, and contract expansions

 - Identify upsell and cross-sell opportunities in collaboration with internal teams

 - Deeply understand the partner’s business model, priorities, and success metrics to proactively propose solutions

 - Ensure alignment between partner expectations and internal capabilities


EXECUTION & CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP

 - Lead the end-to-end execution of strategic initiatives, coordinating across Product, Engineering, Operations, Risk, and Marketing teams

 - Translate business needs into clear, actionable plans and ensure timely, high-quality delivery

 - Prioritize initiatives based on business impact, feasibility, and resource allocation

 - Act as the escalation point for complex issues, driving resolution across teams


PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT & INSIGHTS

 - Define, track, and analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure account success

 - Generate insights and recommendations to improve performance, customer experience, and operational efficiency

 - Monitor risks and proactively implement mitigation strategies

 - Provide regular updates to senior leadership on performance, risks, and opportunities


OPERATIONAL EXCELLENCE & CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT

 - Identify and drive process improvements to enhance scalability and efficiency

 - Promote best practices in execution, stakeholder management, and cross-functional collaboration

 - Lead retrospectives and continuous improvement efforts across initiatives

 - Ensure high standards of organization, documentation, and accountability




JOB REQUIREMENTS

 - 8+ years of experience in account management, business development, consulting, or related roles, with a strong focus on enterprise clients

 - Proven experience owning and growing large, complex accounts with senior executive exposure

 - Strong commercial acumen, including expe

notes: Kueski is a fintech company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles, indicating a strong engineering core but also a significant presence of business and marketing roles.
